key_themes: Strong Q1 2026 earnings beat with record revenue and EPS surprise
Widespread analyst upgrades with numerous price target raises to $139–$160
Progress on restructuring, Services momentum, and Markets strength
Significant capital returns via $6.3B buybacks and reaffirmed RoTCE targets
Positive 2026 outlook including NII growth and efficiency gains
summary: Citigroup delivered robust Q1 2026 results, reporting $24.6 billion in revenue (up 14% YoY and a decade high) and net income of $5.8 billion (up 42%), with EPS of $3.06 beating estimates of $2.63.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/citigroup-c-valuation-check-strong-170429401.html)[[2]](https://public.com/stocks/c/earnings) This strong performance, combined with progress on its transformation, wealth management growth, and Markets franchise strength, triggered immediate analyst enthusiasm—including multiple price target increases to the $139–$160 range and reaffirmed Buy ratings with ~20–30% implied upside.[[3]](https://www.cnn.com/markets/stocks/C) Investor and social media sentiment is clearly bullish, highlighting the earnings crush for options traders, Services as a “crown jewel,” and the company being on track for its 10–11% RoTCE target while returning substantial capital to shareholders. Macro risks remain but are currently outweighed by operational momentum and valuation appeal.[[4]](https://seekingalpha.com/article/4891354-citigroup-still-cheap-following-its-q1-2026-earnings)
sources: 20+ recent news articles and earnings reports (April 2026), 25+ X posts analyzed (primarily post-earnings reaction from April 1–18, 2026)
